aiRoleDefinitions.title: Data Organization & Pre-processing
aiRoleDefinitions.description: Before you even type a search, AI (typically traditional machine learning models) **organizes and enriches academic content behind the scenes**. Tasks include disambiguating author names, classifying topics, assigning controlled vocabularies, and mapping citation networks. This builds the structured foundation that makes accurate searching possible.

aiRoleDefinitions.title: Query Interpretation & Search Strategy Generation
aiRoleDefinitions.description: When you type a question in natural language, the AI (an LLM) **translates it into a complex, structured Boolean/keyword search string** (adding synonyms, AND/OR operators, and date ranges). This optimized string is then **fed into a traditional database to retrieve exact keyword matches**.

aiRoleDefinitions.title: Semantic & Hybrid Retrieval
aiRoleDefinitions.description: Instead of relying solely on exact keyword matches, the AI (specifically, transformer-based encoder models, e.g. BERT) **converts your query and the database's papers into numerical representations** (i.e. vector embeddings). This allows the system to find papers that are conceptually similar to your question, even if they use entirely different words. **Hybrid search** combines this conceptual understanding (semantic search) with traditional keyword matching to deliver the most accurate and relevant results.

aiRoleDefinitions.title: Classification & Relevance Reranking
aiRoleDefinitions.description: Once an initial list of papers is retrieved, the AI (an LLM) reads their metadata (titles and abstracts) to **categorize and grade them**. It might classify the study design (e.g. RCT, systematic review), determine whether the paper supports or contradicts a specific claim, and **rerank the results** so the most contextually relevant papers appear at the very top.

aiRoleDefinitions.title: Single-Document Summarization
aiRoleDefinitions.description: After the paper has been retrieved, the AI (an LLM) helps you understand that specific paper by **generating a summary or explanation** of it (e.g. TL;DRs, per-paper conclusions).

aiRoleDefinitions.title: Sequential Single-Document Data Summarization (Table Extraction)
aiRoleDefinitions.description: The AI (an LLM) **extracts key data** from multiple individual documents and organizes it into a **synthesis table or matrix**, making it useful for evidence synthesis.

aiRoleDefinitions.title: Multi-Document Synthesis (RAG)
aiRoleDefinitions.description: Using **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)**, the AI (an LLM) reads across multiple, the top retrieved papers (chunks of texts) and **writes a new, synthesized answer to your specific question**, grounded in those sources and supported with inline citations.

aiRoleDefinitions.title: Agentic Research Orchestration
aiRoleDefinitions.description: Instead of doing a single search-and-summarize action, the AI (an LLM) acts as an **autonomous research assistant**. It is given control over a toolkit and **iteratively plans, executes, and adapts** its strategy across multiple steps. It decides which tools to use, evaluates intermediate results, and refines its search queries as it learns more. This architecture orchestrates all the previous roles (query generation, semantic search, reranking, and RAG) into a continuous loop, enabling complex, comprehensive research workflows.

stages.title: Scholarly & Open Databases
stages.desc: Traditional academic databases and open scholarly platforms provide the foundational infrastructure for literature discovery — through keyword/Boolean search, and open APIs.

groups.label: Scholarly Databases
groups.fmt: Keyword / Boolean search → list of articles, books, conference papers, patents

groups.label: Open Databases
groups.fmt: Keyword / API search →  list of works, or metadata

stages.title: General LLMs & AI Search Engines
stages.desc: General-purpose large language models become available for research support, offered directly or through aggregator platforms. AI-powered search engines emerge that synthesize answers from live web sources with inline citations.

groups.label: LLM Aggregators
groups.fmt: Natural language prompt → AI response via aggregated model access

groups.label: AI Search Engines
groups.fmt: Natural language query → AI answer with inline citations from live web sources

stages.title: AI-Powered Research Tools
stages.desc: Specialized AI tools evolve along multiple branches: AI-native research tools answer research questions grounded in academic literature, scholarly databases add AI assistants for in-database search and/or synthesis, and deep search tools perform iterative, exhaustive paper discovery.

groups.label: AI-Native Research Assistants
groups.fmt: Natural language question → AI summary with inline citations from broad academic indexes or user uploaded documents

groups.label: Scholarly Databases (RAG-Based)
groups.fmt: Natural language question → AI summary with citations scoped to a specific subscribed database

groups.label: Scholarly Databases (Enhanced Search)
groups.fmt: Natural language question → ranked search results within a subscribed database

groups.label: Deep Search
groups.fmt: Natural language question → multi-round iterative search → ranked results with relevance explanations

stages.title: Deep Research & Agentic Workflows
stages.desc: AI research agents combine deep, multi-round retrieval with autonomous report generation — academic tools produce structured literature reviews/reports with full citations, general web agents synthesize from live sources, and MCP-powered agentic workflows enable AI assistants to query databases directly within conversation.

groups.label: Deep Research (Academic)
groups.fmt: Natural language question → autonomous multi-round search → full research report with academic citations

groups.label: Deep Research (General)
groups.fmt: Natural language question → autonomous web browsing → long-form report with web source citations

groups.label: Agentic Workflows + MCP
groups.fmt: Natural language question/instruction → direct database query via MCP → results synthesized seamlessly in conversation

stages.title: Post-Generation Mapping
stages.desc: Library discovery layers bridge AI-generated outputs back to verified institutional collections — mapping LLM answers to library-subscribed sources

groups.label: Library Discovery Layer
groups.fmt: LLM generated answer → mapped to library collections → verified with institutional holdings

tools.name: Google Scholar
tools.about: Largest academic search engine, helps users find scholarly articles, books, conference papers, and court opinions across many disciplines.
tools.unique:
**Free**
**Largest index of scholarly literature**
tools.coverage: Estimated 300M+ documents across all academic disciplines, source types, and languages.
tools.limitations:
No filtering for **peer-reviewed content**
**Metadata** is inconsistent
**Ranking algorithm** is not transparent
**Advanced search operators** are limited

tools.name: Google Scholar Labs
tools.about: An experimental AI-powered search feature within Google Scholar that breaks complex research questions into sub-queries, evaluates papers individually, and provides AI-generated rationales for each paper’s relevance.
tools.unique:
**Free and built in** – Included in Google Scholar at no extra cost.
**Largest academic index** – Use Google Scholar’s broad coverage across publishers, disciplines, and grey literature.
**AI explanation for each paper** – Give an AI-generated note for every result explaining why each result is relevant to the research question.
**LLM-based relevance screening** – Screen up to 300 top results to improve precision compared with traditional Google Scholar keyword ranking.
**Follow-up questions** – Support follow-up questions in the same session.
**Keep Google Scholar features** – Retain PDF links, citation counts, and library link resolver support.
**Includes books** - Shows Google Books results alongside journal articles.
tools.similar:
Ai2 Asta - Find Papers (deep search)
Consensus (deep search)
Semantic Scholar (AI enhanced search with TL;DR summaries)
tools.limitations:
**Limited screening depth** – Despite the large index, it reviews only up to 300 papers per session and stops after finding 50 relevant ones.
**No cross-paper synthesis** – Provide a ranked, annotated list rather than a narrative summary.
**No session saving** – Results are lost when the session ends unless the session link is shared and retained.
**Limited transparency** – Unclear which model is used, how many sub-queries are generated, or what text is screened.
tools.ai_role: LLM analyzes queries, generate multiple sub-queries, and evaluate/rank each paper's relevance with a generated rationale; focus on deep search and no cross-paper synthesis.
tools.privacy: No specific mentioning of AI training on user data in the privacy policy.

tools.name: Web of Science
tools.about: Clarivate's largest abstract and citation database of peer-reviewed literature.
tools.coverage: WoS Core Collection covers 22k+ peer-reviewed journals in sciences, social sciences, arts & humanities.
tools.privacy: Clarivate may anonymize, aggregate, or de-identify personal data to create non-personal data for use in AI systems, including generative AI, to improve services, develop features, conduct research, and evaluate effectiveness. All personal data will remain subject to applicable data protection laws.

tools.name: Web of Science - Smart Search
tools.about: Developed by Clarivate, an AI-powered tool that simplifies search by allowing users to input natural language queries, focuses on improving search results, and does not generate direct answers using RAG. It’s bundled with existing Web of Science subscription without additional cost.
tools.unique:
**Hybrid search & transparent filtering** - Combine keyword and semantic search, allow filtering results by “Boolean”, “Semantic”, or “Combined”.
**Reproducibility for Boolean results** – Can filter to “Boolean” to retrieve the same number of results as a traditional keyword search in Web of Science.
**Enhanced recall** – Can uncover relevant studies that traditional keyword searches might miss.
**Multilingual search capability** - support queries in multiple languages.
tools.coverage: Searches exclusively the Web of Science Core Collection, an editorially selected, high-quality curated database covering 21k+ peer-reviewed journals across sciences, social sciences, arts, and humanities, plus conference proceedings and books, from 1900 to present.
tools.limitations:
**Limited query transparency** – Unlike WoS Research Assistant, it does not show the converted Boolean search query.
**Non-deterministic query generation** – The Boolean query generated by the LLM can vary, which reduces the reproducibility of the search strategy.
**Lower interpretability and reproducibility in semantic results** – Although the Boolean results are highly reproducible, the semantic search component is less transparent and predictable.
**Unclear reranking mechanism** – The final ranking of results from keyword and semantic search is harder to interpret.
**No generative summaries** – Does not generate direct answers or summaries.
tools.ai_role: Use AI to interpret natural language queries and use vector embeddings to enhance retrieval through a hybrid search model, focusing on improving search query results

tools.name: Web of Science Research Assistant
tools.about: Developed by Clarivate, an AI-powered tool built on the Web of Science Core Collection, that leverages its multidisciplinary citation database for literature review, topic exploration, and journal discovery through interactive multi-step workflows. Unlike WoS Smart Search, it does perform RAG to synthesize answers.
tools.unique:
**High transparency** - displays the LLM-generated Boolean search query with a direct link to run it in the standard WoS interface
**Knowledge graph integration** – leverages WoS's structured knowledge graph to expand queries with semantically related terms
**Dual query modes** – offers “Summary Queries” for conceptual overviews and “Document Search Queries” for specific paper retrieval
**Agentic AI guides** – interactive multi-step task guides for “literature review” and “topic exploration” that iterate with user needs
**Grounded exclusively in WoS Core Collection** – all responses are derived from quality-vetted content
**Unique visualizations** – generates interactive co-citation networks, topic maps, trend graphs, and co-author maps
**Multilingual input and output** – accepts queries in multiple languages and returns answers in the query language
tools.similar:
Primo Research Assistant (Boolean generation, lexical search and synthesize)
Scopus AI (RAG on top of a curated citation database)
tools.limitations:
**Additional cost** – requires an additional cost beyond the base WoS subscription
**Lexical search rather than semantic search**
**Non-deterministic query generation** – the LLM-generated Boolean query can vary, leading to moderate reproducibility for the search strategy
**Influence of embedding-based reranking** – the final order of results is influenced by a less interpretable reranking step
**RAG limited to abstracts** – does not use full-text even when available
tools.ai_role: Use an LLM to convert natural language questions into Boolean search and synthesizes across the top 8 retrieved abstracts to produce narrative answers

tools.name: Nexus
tools.about: Announced by Clarivate in Jan 2026, an academic assistant that functions as an embedded bridge layer, connecting general AI tools and web environments to institutional library collections, citation indexes, and services. Full release planned for Q3 2026.
tools.unique:
**Embedded library bridge** - Operate as a browser extension to integrate library content into general AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude) and web workflows.
**Real-time citation verification** - Check AI-generated references against trusted scholarly indexes.
**Full-text access** - Provide one-click access to licensed content.
**Library configurability** - Reflect each institution’s branding and priorities.
**Library visibility** - Surface relevant library services during AI research.
**Built-in analytics** - Track usage and behavior to guide collection decisions.
tools.coverage: Draws on Clarivate's academic indexes and collections, including Web of Science citation index (90M+ records), Primo and Summon Central Discovery Index (hundreds of millions of items), and ProQuest collections, with coverage aligning with institutional subscriptions and including open access content.
tools.limitations: Not yet released; to be confirmed upon launch
tools.ai_role: AI is applied after search retrieval. It does not generate or synthesize content; instead, it processes the LLM output and links it to library holdings.

tools.name: Scopus
tools.about: Elsevier's largest abstract and citation database of peer-reviewed literature, now integrated with an AI query builder.
tools.coverage: Covers 29k+ peer-reviewed journals, conference proceedings, and book series across sciences, social sciences, arts, and humanities.
tools.ai_role: Use AI query builder to formulate and refine Boolean queries from natural language input; no generative summary produced.
tools.privacy: “Our enterprise agreements with AWS, Microsoft Azure, OpenAI, and Anthropic include zero-retention contracts, so your prompts and documents are never used for large language model development. Elsevier also does not use customer data in our private cloud environments for this purpose.”

tools.name: Scopus AI (AI Discovery)
tools.about:
Developed by Elsevier, an AI research tool that combines the Scopus bibliographic database with LLM to help researchers quickly discover, summarize, and navigate scholarly literature.

Note: Starting Apr 2026, Scopus AI has been migrated to LeapSpace, which unifies the capabilities of Scopus AI and ScienceDirect AI into a single workspace.
tools.unique:
**Transparency** - displays query optimization steps and the generated Boolean query.
**RAG Fusion technology** - combines hybrid search (LLM-generated Boolean + parallel vector search) with Reciprocal Rank Fusion to rerank results, improving recall and precision
**Topic Summary and Expanded Summary** - produces structured summaries with references and confidence-in-relevance indicators
**Foundational documents** - mines the full Scopus database to surface high-impact papers cited by papers used in summaries
**Concept Map** - generates interactive visual maps from keywords in abstracts to show topic relationships.
**Deep Research** - agentic AI feature that develops a detailed research plan, iteratively searches Scopus, and produces comprehensive downloadable reports
tools.similar:
ScienceDirect AI (Elsevier product with full-text access but narrower corpus)
LeapSpace (successor of ScienceDirect AI and Scopus AI)
Primo Research Assistant (hybrid search + multi-document RAG for library discovery)
Web of Science Research Assistant (RAG over a curated citation database)
tools.limitations:
**Additional cost** – requires an additional cost beyond the base Scopus subscription
**Abstract-only access** – works only from titles and abstracts
**Limited transparency in final ranking** – while the Boolean query is shown, the final result order from RAG Fusion/vector reranking is harder to interpret or reproduce
**Hallucination risk in RAG summaries** – susceptible to subtle misrepresentation of evidence despite strong guardrails
**Retraction/correction awareness** – depends on Scopus metadata being updated; may not always flag retracted papers in generated summaries
tools.ai_role: Combines LLM-generated Boolean queries with parallel vector search to retrieve relevant content. It synthesizes insights across multiple abstracts to generate paragraph-length summaries and deep research reports.

tools.name: Scopus AI - Deep Research
tools.about:
Developed by Elsevier, an AI research tool that combines the Scopus bibliographic database with LLM to help researchers quickly discover, summarize, and navigate scholarly literature. The Deep Research feature leverages agentic AI for complex workflows. It creates a detailed research plan, searches Scopus’ curated literature, refines its strategy as new insights emerge, and delivers synthesized findings in a in-depth, downloadable report. 

Note: Starting Apr 2026, Scopus AI has been migrated to LeapSpace, which unifies the capabilities of Scopus AI and ScienceDirect AI into a single workspace.
tools.ai_role: Generative LLM performs iterative query expansion and synthesis, grounded in Scopus's curated scholarly index.

tools.name: ScienceDirect AI
tools.about:
Developed by Elsevier, a AI research tool built on ScienceDirect (the world's largest platform of peer-reviewed full-text research) that enables researchers to search, summarise, and compare insights from its content. 

Note: Starting Apr 2026, Scopus AI has been migrated to LeapSpace, which unifies the capabilities of Scopus AI and ScienceDirect AI into a single workspace.
tools.unique:
**Full-text grounding** - Provide deep, passage-level access to full-text Elsevier content and link every AI-generated claim to a specific source passage.
**Hybrid search** - Combine semantic search and keyword search.
**Ask ScienceDirect AI** - Offer a standalone chatbot that generates article summaries with citations.
**Reading Assistant** - Enable conversational exploration of a single article or book chapter, with every response citing specific source passages.
**Compare Experiments** - Present a structured table that break down experiments across retrieved articles for side-by-side comparison (e.g. purpose, methods, conclusions).
tools.similar: LeapSpace (successor of ScienceDirect AI and Scopus AI)
tools.coverage: Indexes 14-18 million full-text peer-reviewed journal articles and book chapters from ScienceDirect, with optional Scopus abstract integration extending coverage to 100M+ records from 7,000+ publishers. It focuses primarily on STEM, health sciences, and social sciences.
tools.limitations:
**Additional cost required** – Require separate subscription from ScienceDirect.
**Elsevier-only full-text corpus** – Cover mainly Elsevier journals, with no indexing of other OA content, preprints, or conference papers outside Elsevier venues.
**Retrieval limit** – Select and summarize only the top 10 articles for each query.
tools.ai_role: Use AI for query-based multi-document summarization in 'Ask ScienceDirect AI' and full-text single-document Q&A in the 'Reading Assistant'. It also supports per-article extraction and structured comparison in 'Compare Experiments'.

tools.name: LeapSpace
tools.about: Elsevier’s next-generation “research-grade AI workspace” launched in Jan 2026, is designed to unify the capabilities of Scopus AI and ScienceDirect AI into a single secure platform which supports the full researcher workflow, from literature discovery and funding identification to collaborator discovery and hypothesis generation.
tools.unique:
**Trust Cards** - Show the exact passage used to support each AI-generated insight for easy verification
**Publisher-neutral full-text corpus** - Covers abstracts and full text from Elsevier and other leading publishers and societies
**Deep research** - Generate in-depth reports that surface emerging patterns, contradictions, and evidence gaps traditional searches often miss
**Integrated funding discovery** - Provide access to funding opportunities government and private funding organizations
**File upload** - Allow upload of up to 5 files per conversation
**Reading assistant** - Enable conversational questioning of a specific full-text article or book chapter
**Compare feature** - Compare articles in a table that highlights goals, methods, results, and experimental details
**Author search** - Explore relevant collaborators, mentors, topic contributors, and cross-disciplinary research connections
tools.coverage: Combines 16M+ peer-reviewed full-text articles and books from Elsevier and licensed subscription and open access articles from other leading publishers and societies, along with 105M+ abstracts from Scopus, covering all major disciplines.
tools.limitations:
**Extra subscription cost on top of Scopus**
**No pre-filtering options**
tools.ai_role: An integration of Scopus AI and ScienceDirect AI features.

tools.name: EBSCOhost
tools.about: EBSCO's research platform that provides access to a vast collection of licensed databases, including peer-reviewed journals, e-books, and historical archives across diverse academic disciplines.
tools.coverage: Covers 11B+ items across academic journals, books, dissertations, conference papers, magazines, and newspapers from thousands of publishers across all academic disciplines, including medicine (MEDLINE), nursing (CINAHL), psychology (PsycINFO), education (ERIC), business (Business Source), literature, etc.

tools.name: EBSCOhost NLS
tools.about: Developed by EBSCO, an opt-in AI-powered search mode built into EBSCOhost platform interfaces. Bundled with existing EBSCO subscriptions at no additional cost.
tools.unique:
**Search query transparency** - Let users see the exact Boolean string generated by the LLM through “Show refined query”
**Conservative, complementary query expansion** - Generate shorter Boolean strings with fewer synonyms while deferring broader concept expansion to EBSCO’s pre-existing Unified Subject Index (USI)
**Unified Subject Index (USI) synergy** - Work in sequence with EBSCO’s USI to automatically expand queries using linked controlled vocabularies and subject headings
**Paired with AI Insights** - Handle query formulation while a companion feature provides fixed-button key insight summaries of individual full-text articles
**Opt-in administrative configuration** - Allow library administrators to enable NLS as the default search mode, offer it as an optional toggle, or disable it entirely
tools.coverage: Covers the full range of EBSCO's databases and EBSCO Discovery Service (EDS) index, which provides access to 11B+ items across academic journals, books, dissertations, conference papers, magazines, and newspapers from thousands of publishers across all academic disciplines, including medicine (MEDLINE), nursing (CINAHL), psychology (PsycINFO licensed), education (ERIC), business (Business Source), literature, etc.
tools.limitations:
**Lexical search rather than semantic search**
Returns search results only, no AI-generated summary
Quality varies across databases
Subscription coverage determines accessible content
tools.ai_role: LLM converts natural language queries to Boolean search queries and runs them through standard keyword search. It does not generate synthesized answers or summaries; “AI Insights” are based on individual article abstracts or metadata.
tools.privacy: NLS query data is not stored or used to train the AI model.

tools.name: IEEE Xplore
tools.about: IEEE's digital library for discovering journal articles, conference papers, and technical standards, across electrical engineering, computer science, and related STEM fields.
tools.coverage: Indexes 7M+ full-text IEEE documents including journals, magazines, transactions, conference proceedings, and standards, while the AI Research Suite also leverages STEM metadata and abstracts from OpenAlex’s 250M+ works. It supports discovery across electrical engineering, computer science, and related STEM fields.
tools.privacy: “IEEE Xplore customer data will never be used to train, fine-tune, or otherwise improve any Large Language Models or related products. AI tools are not permitted to access IEEE Xplore customer data or usage.”

tools.name: IEEE Xplore AI Search
tools.about: Developed by IEEE, a beta-stage overlay that adds GenAI capabilities to the IEEE Xplore corpus, searching IEEE full text, and returning AI-generated overviews with inline citations.
tools.unique:
**Curated STEM content** - Draw exclusively from IEEE and curated STEM sources.
**Publication coverage** - Include IEEE publications and select non-IEEE STEM publishers through OpenAlex metadata enrichment.
**AI-generated summaries** - Provide time-saving overviews of individual papers and topics.
**In-context definitions** - Explain unfamiliar technical terms directly within the search interface.
**In-platform annotations** - Enable researchers to annotate content without leaving the platform.
tools.similar:
Scopus AI
Primo Research Assistant
O'Reilly AI (for domain-specific RAG)
tools.limitations:
**STEM-focused coverage** - limit results mainly to IEEE and curated STEM content, with weaker coverage of humanities, social sciences, clinical medicine, grey literature, and non-indexed sources.
**Search recall constraints** - return top 30 results rather than exhaustive Boolean matches
**Beta-stage limitations** – newly added content in IEEE Xplore may not be indexed on AI search results.
tools.ai_role: Use LLM to understand natural-language queries, search for relevant IEEE full-text content and OpenAlex STEM metadata, and then generate a synthesized answer with inline citations from multiple sources.

tools.name: O'Reilly
tools.about: O’Reilly’s learning platform that provides access to over ebooks, video tutorials, and interactive sandboxes covering technology, business, and software development.
tools.unique: Answers are grounded in authoritative technical manuals and programming guides
tools.coverage: Indexes 70,000+ books, videos, online courses, and live training sessions primarily covering software development, data science, AI/ML, cloud, cybersecurity, UX design, and business technology, with content exclusively from O'Reilly Media's editorially curated library.
tools.limitations:
Limited to O'Reilly content
Technology and business focus only
Institutional subscription required

tools.name: O'Reilly AI
tools.about: O’Reilly’s AI Answers - an AI-powered learning platform feature that use RAG to deliver responses grounded in O’Reilly’s database of technical books and videos. It also offer an MCP server that let AI agents programmatically search O’Reilly content.
tools.unique:
**“AI Answers”** - Provide answers using only books, videos, and courses within O’Reilly platform
**Multilingual query support** - Accept questions in multi-language while returning answers in English
**Official MCP server** - Provide programmatic access to O’Reilly content and metadata for AI tools, agents, and RAG pipelines directly from tools like Cursor and VS Code
tools.similar:
Statista AI (for domain-specific RAG)
IEEE Xplore AI Search (for domain-specific RAG)
tools.coverage: Indexes 70,000+ books, videos, online courses, and live training sessions primarily covering software development, data science, AI/ML, cloud, cybersecurity, UX design, and business technology, with content exclusively from O'Reilly Media's editorially curated library.
tools.limitations:
**Content scope** - cover only O’Reilly-published material, with no access to peer-reviewed journals, conference papers, or academic databases.
**Response coverage** - remain limited to O’Reilly’s catalog, so niche or cutting-edge research topics may be missing or outdated.
**Institutional subscription required**
tools.ai_role: RAG-based synthesis tool that retrieves, ranks, validates, and trims relevant content from technical book chapters and video transcripts to answer user questions.

tools.name: JSTOR
tools.about: JSTOR is a non-profit digital library that provides access to a vast archive of academic journal articles, books, and primary sources.
tools.coverage: Covers JSTOR's corpus of 13M+ academic items, including journals, books, and images across 75+ disciplines, primarily humanities, social sciences, and arts, with the AI research tool using only JSTOR content.

tools.name: JSTOR AI Research Tool
tools.about: An AI-powered assistant embedded directly on JSTOR's content pages that help users assess article relevance, discover related content, and ask natural-language questions about the material. Developed by ITHAKA and available free to JSTOR-participating institutions.
tools.unique: Improves relevancy without requiring exact keywords; adds prompts relating queries to document content
tools.limitations:
Archival focus with 'moving wall' for recent issues
Returns search results only, no AI-generated summary
Less STEM coverage
tools.ai_role: Uses embeddings for semantic retrieval to improve relevancy ranking beyond keyword matching; no generative synthesis.

tools.name: Factiva
tools.about: A news and data platform by Dow Jones, serving as a primary source for news and business information.
tools.coverage: Indexes 33k+ global news and business information sources, delivering 400k+ new articles daily from various publications across 200 countries and 33 languages, with 8,000+ sources explicitly licensed for GenAI use.

tools.name: Factiva AI
tools.about: A primary source for news and business information, with GenAI features including “Factiva Smart Summary”, powered by Gemini models to synthesize licensed global news and business content.
tools.unique:
**Semantic search** - Enable intuitive search through embedding-based retrieval and API-based AI research solutions.
**Factiva Smart Summary** - Provide Smart Summary to synthesize licensed global news and business content.
**Global multilingual coverage** - Cover 33,000+ news and business sources across 200 countries and 33 languages.
tools.coverage: Thousands of global news sources, business publications, and company reports.
tools.limitations:
**Uneven regional coverage** - Vary in depth across local and non-English news markets.
**Limited GenAI coverage** - Restrict Smart Summary to licensed sources cleared for AI use rather than the full Factiva corpus.
tools.ai_role: AI-assisted summarization and entity extraction from business news.

tools.name: Statista
tools.about: A global business intelligence platform that provides statistics, market reports, and consumer insights from a wide range of reputable sources across industries worldwide.
tools.coverage: Covers 1.5M+ statistics and quantitative business and market data from more than 22,500 sources across 170 industries and 150+ countries, including research firms, government agencies, industry associations, and company reports. It primarily provides statistics, charts, and market reports rather than academic literature.

tools.name: Statista AI
tools.about: Statista offers a generative AI assistant as an add-on feature. It uses LLMs and Cohere semantic search to help users find information in Statista’s database of statistics, infographics, market reports, and topic pages. It also offers MCP server that lets AI agents programmatically search the Statista’s content (2025-11).
tools.unique:
**Grounded proprietary data search** - Ground all answers in Statista’s human-verified data rather than the open web.
**AI-powered natural language search** - Use Claude, Cohere, and semantic search to answer plain-language queries.
**Inline citations and source transparency** - Provide direct links to the underlying charts and reports for verification.
**Structured and digestible outputs** - Return narrative summaries, top statistics tables, recommendations, and TL;DR overviews.
**Clear separation of AI and source data** - Distinguish AI-generated summaries from original verified statistics.
**Statista MCP** - Enable conversational exploration of Statista’s statistical content.
tools.similar:
IEEE Xplore AI Search (for domain-specific RAG)
O'Reilly AI (for domain-specific RAG)
tools.coverage: Covers quantitative business and market data from 22,500+ sources, including research firms, government agencies, industry associations, and company reports, across 170 industries and 150+ countries. It primarily delivers statistics, charts, and market reports rather than qualitative academic literature.
tools.limitations:
**Limited content scope** - Cover quantitative market and business data rather than academic or scientific literature.
**Restricted source retrieval** - Return a maximum of 10 cited sources per query, which may be narrower than standard keyword search.
**Non-deterministic outputs** - Produce varying results and cited sources for the same prompt across sessions.
**No saved session history** - Do not retain prompts or history in institutional license sessions.
**Language-dependent quality** - Perform best in English, with variable quality across other languages.
tools.ai_role: LLM synthesizes answers based on retrieved statistical reports and data points.

tools.name: Statista MCP
tools.about: MCP integration for accessing Statista's statistics and market data within compatible AI assistants.
tools.unique: Brings quantitative market data directly into flexible AI chat environments
tools.limitations:
Requires compatible MCP client
Limited to Statista content
tools.ai_role: LLM orchestrates query formulation and synthesizes retrieved statistical data.

tools.name: CNKI
tools.about: The largest Chinese academic database covering journals, dissertations, conference papers, newspapers, and yearbooks.
tools.coverage: Covers 380M+ documents, including journals, dissertations, conference papers, newspapers, and books from the 1990s to the present.

tools.name: CNKI AI
tools.about: An AI-powered research assistant built on CNKI, the largest Chinese academic literature database, that enable users to search, synthesize, and access insights from extensive Chinese-language academic resources through natural language queries.
tools.unique:
**Chinese-language academic discovery** - Enable AI-driven semantic search and concept-based retrieval across Chinese scholarly content.
**AI-enhanced retrieval** - Upgrade keyword search with semantic retrieval, conceptual summaries, and knowledge graph support.
**AI research assistant** - Provide Q&A-enhanced retrieval, topic-level Q&A, and full-database question answering.
tools.limitations:
**Primarily Chinese-language content** - Limit utility for researchers who do not work with Chinese sources.
**Limited AI transparency** - Provide little public detail on specific AI features and underlying models.
**Subscription required** - Additional cost required on top of existing CNKI subscription
tools.ai_role: LLM synthesizes answers grounded in CNKI's vast Chinese academic corpus.

tools.name: Patsnap
tools.about: An academic and patent search platform that bridges the gap between scientific literature and real-world innovation by mapping millions of research papers directly to global patent data.
tools.coverage: Covers ~210M patents across 174 jurisdictions, integrating scientific literature, regulatory filings, and startup activity data, with particular depth in life sciences, chemistry, materials, electronics, and mechanical engineering.

tools.name: Patsnap AI
tools.unique:
**Patsnap AI Agent** - Use PatsnapGPT, RAG, and CoT reasoning to deliver verified, cited answers and automate multi-step tasks such as novelty reporting and competitor monitoring
**Expert AI search** - Combine semantic understanding with keyword and Boolean logic while giving users transparent, editable query control
**Semantic patent search** - Use NLP and transformer-based embeddings to identify conceptually similar prior art, retrieve and rank up to 1,000 results by relevance
**Hiro AI Assistant** - Provide conversational patent analysis, technical explanations, and instant reporting, including life sciences support
**AI tagging in Workspace** - Classify and organize documents with AI-driven tagging that supports team collaboration and project consistency.
tools.coverage: Patsnap's 150M+ global patents from 100+ countries.
tools.limitations:
**Subscription required ** – Require a paid Patsnap subscription, with AI features incurring additional credit-based costs
**Expert review required** - Require human validation and not serve as the sole authority for high-stakes IP decisions
tools.ai_role: Use a domain-trained model to interpret natural-language prompt, perform both keyword and semantic searches, retrieve and rank relevant technical documents, and generate precise, explainable answers with citations under user-approved guardrails.

tools.name: SciFinder
tools.about: A premier research discovery tool from the American Chemical Society that provides access to the world's most comprehensive collection of substances, reactions, and peer-reviewed references in chemistry and related sciences.
tools.coverage: Covers 204M+ organic and inorganic substances reported in the literature since the early 1800s, 69M protein and nucleic acid sequences, and about 150M reactions. The literature base spans 59M+ records from publications dating back to the early 1800s.

tools.name: SciFinder AI
tools.about: A premier research discovery tool from the American Chemical Society that combines access to the world’s most comprehensive collection of substances, reactions, and peer-reviewed references in chemistry and related sciences with AI features such as natural-language search, smart query interpretation, literature summarization, and conversational scientific assistance.
tools.unique:
**Natural-language scientific search** - Understand multi-word concepts and reaction roles, enabling plain scientific phrasing
**Substance and identifier recognition** - Recognize substance name variations, drawings, DOIs, patents, and titles to direct users to the correct chemical identifiers or source documents
**Traceable summarization** - Synthesize key themes from the most relevant literature, links them to supporting sources, and allow to download a PDF and trace back to the conversation.
**“CAS Newton” Agentic Assistant** (2026-04) - Provide a secure conversational AI agent to manage multi-step research workflows and refine complex inquiries
**Expert-curated data grounding** - Power AI capabilities exclusively through the curated CAS Content Collection to reduce hallucination risk
tools.limitations:
**Retrieval limit in AI summary** - Generate summaries using only up to the top 20 results
**CAS-only content scope** - Rely exclusively on CAS content for retrieval and summary generation
**Daily query limit** - Limit usage to 3 questions per day as of 2026-04
tools.ai_role: Use AI to interpret natural language queries for semantic retrieval and synthesize top relevant results using RAG.

tools.name: PubMed
tools.about: The National Library of Medicine’s primary biomedical literature database and the gold standard for biomedical searching, combining controlled MeSH vocabulary and systematic review filters with AI- and machine-learning–enhanced retrieval and indexing.
tools.coverage: Covers 40M+ citations and abstracts from Medline and additional life science titles, with full text for ~8M articles freely available via PubMed Central (PMC), focusing exclusively on biomedicine, nursing, pharmacy, dentistry, veterinary medicine, and healthcare.

tools.name: PubMed - AI Search
tools.unique:
**Free & comprehensive biomedical coverage** - offer publicly funded, universally accessible biomedical literature at no cost
**AI-enhanced search and ranking** - improve retrieval with Best Match, query suggestions, spell correction, and author disambiguation
**ML-assisted indexing and term mapping** - apply automated MeSH indexing and Automatic Term Mapping to expand recall while preserve precision
**PubMed MCP server** (2025-11) - allow LLM agents to run structured PubMed searches and access millions of PMC full-text articles
tools.similar:
Medline
Web of Science Smart Search (retrieval-only, no synthesis)
Semantic Scholar
tools.limitations:
**Biomedical focus only**
**No AI summaries** – Remain a retrieval and indexing platform rather than a content-generation tool.
**No semantic search** - Rely on lexical search.
**MCP access limit** – Full-text access via MCP is limited to open access PMC articles and require PMID-based retrieval rather than natural language full-text search.
tools.ai_role: Use AI to expand queries using MeSH concepts and biomedical entity recognition; no generative synthesis.

tools.name: PubMed MCP
tools.about: The National Library of Medicine’s primary biomedical literature database and the gold standard for biomedical searching, combining controlled MeSH vocabulary and systematic review filters with AI- and machine-learning–enhanced retrieval and indexing. Also offer a PubMed MCP server for LLM agents such as Claude to search, check MeSH headings, and retrieve PMC full text.
tools.similar:
Wiley Scholar Gateway MCP
Scite MCP

tools.name: Scite Assistant
tools.about: An AI-powered research platform that answers questions with citation-grounded responses from scholarly literature and classifies citations as supporting, contrasting, or mentioning.
tools.unique:
**Citation statements** - ground AI responses in article full text for easy verification
**Smart Citations** - classify citations as supporting, contrasting, or mentioning, with full in-text snippets rather than simple counts
**Pre-filtering** - choose models, set the number of publications reviewed, filter by year, publication type, journal, etc.
**Search transparency** - show the full search strategy and all consulted sources
**Output formats** - support both chat and table/literature matrix views
**MCP server** (Feb 2026) - enable direct integration with Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, and other MCP-compatible AI tool
tools.similar:
Consensus (literature search + summary)
Elicit (literature search + summary)
SciSpace (literature search + summary)
tools.coverage: Covers 280 million+ papers, books, preprints, etc. across all disciplines and includes 1 billion+ Smart Citation statements extracted from full-text articles. Its content comes from both direct publisher indexing agreements and major open access sources.
tools.limitations:
**Search** - rely on keyword-based Boolean search rather than semantic search
**Model variability** - produce different, non-deterministic results across models
**Summary scope** - draw summaries from the top 10–20 results (depending on settings and model choice), not all consulted sources
**Citation faithfulness** - cite real sources, but generated claims still require verification
**Pricing** - requires a paid subscription for full functionality
tools.ai_role: A RAG-based system that synthesizes across citation statements from multiple papers to answer a query.
tools.privacy: User data may be used for service improvement, analytics, and advertising. No explicit opt-out for AI training.

tools.name: Scite MCP
tools.about: MCP integration for citation context analysis within Claude — showing how papers support, contrast, or mention findings.
tools.unique: Allows custom AI agents to perform automated fact-checking using Scite's citation classification
tools.coverage: Scite's 1.2B+ citation statements from 35M+ full-text articles.
tools.limitations:
Requires Claude with MCP
Limited to Scite-indexed full-text
tools.ai_role: LLM orchestrates retrieval of citation statements to validate claims dynamically.

tools.name: Elicit - Find Papers
tools.about: An AI research assistant designed to automate and accelerate scientific literature review and systematic review workflows.
tools.unique:
**Query suggestions** – evaluate search queries and suggest improvements
**Hybrid search** - combine keyword and semantic search using SPLADE
**Literature extraction table** - add custom columns (e.g. methodology) to extract and compare across papers
**Research Reports** - generate automated research overviews grounded in systematic review principles
**Systematic Review** (Pro tier) - provide a guided, automated workflow that mirrors systematic review methodology; support strict inclusion and exclusion criteria (2025-12); Boolean keyword search (2026-01); for Pro users, allow up to 5,000 papers per review and 1,000 results per search (2026-02)
**Research Agents** (Pro tier) - powered by Claude-Opus-4.6, automate multi-step workflows for competitive landscape analysis, broad topic exploration, and research landscape mapping (2025-12)
**Browser extension** - enable automatic full-text PDF download through library subscriptions (2025-10)
**API access** - programmatic access to Elicit content, for Pro and Team users (2026-03)
tools.similar:
SciSpace (literature extraction table, systematic review)
Scite Assistant (literature search + summary)
Consensus (literature search + summary)
Rayyan (systematic review screening)
tools.coverage: Covers 138M+ academic papers across all disciplines and 545,000+ clinical trials. Draws from Semantic Scholar, PubMed, and OpenAlex, with strong open access full-text coverage and regular updates.
tools.limitations:
**Free tier limits** - no data export, max. 2 custom columns, and 2 monthly reports
**Full-text access limits** - rely on abstracts for most paywalled papers, which can reduce extraction quality
**Retrieval bias** - semantic search without citation weighting tend to favor newer papers over seminal older ones 
**Citation faithfulness** - cite real sources, but generated claims still require verification
**Non-deterministic results** - produce different paper sets and summaries across repeated runs of the same query
**Extraction reliability** - contain errors in AI-generated extractions and summaries, so verification still required
tools.ai_role: Elicit uses SPLADE for retrieval, a learned sparse embedding method that goes beyond lexical search. It also emphasizes extraction of data rather than pure generation to enhance reliability.
tools.privacy: Usage data will be collected for “business purposes”. No explicit opt-out for AI training.

tools.name: Elicit - Systematic Review
tools.ai_role: Generative LLMs handle data extraction and multi-document synthesis over iteratively retrieved papers.

tools.name: Consensus
tools.about: An AI-powered academic search engine for evidence-based research that helps users find and assess scientific literature.
tools.unique:
**Consensus Meter** - unique visual feature to categorize retrieved papers into Yes/No/Possibly/Mixed to show the distribution of scientific consensus for a yes/no question
**Study Snapshot** – a snapshot of study, extracting population, methods/study design, sample size, outcomes, and results for each paper
**Study classification** – label search results by study design, journal reputation, etc.
**Medical mode** - allow search within top medical journals and clinical guidelines for evidence-based medicine queries
**Pre- & post-filtering** - offer granular filters for study design, sample size, field, country, journal quartile, citation count, publication year, and preprint/OA exclusion
**LibKey integration** - provide direct access to full-text articles through library subscriptions for verification
**Deep Search** - show a transparent, PRISMA-like review flow by screening around 1,000 papers, selecting the top 50, and displaying step-by-step counts of identified, screened, and included studies
**Scholar Agent** - powered by GPT-5, enable multi-step iterative research through specialized sub-agents (2025-10)
**MCP server** (Mar 2026) - enable direct integration with Claude, ChatGPT, and other MCP-compatible AI tool
**Integration with bibliographic reference tools** - Import Zotero collections and papers via DOI/RIS/BibTeX, generate bibliographies in any citation style, and export to LaTeX (2026-04)
tools.similar:
Elicit (literature search, systematic review)
SciSpace (literature search, systematic review)
Scite Assistant (literature search + summary)
Undermind (agentic deep search)
tools.coverage: Covers 220M+ papers and preprints across disciplines, primarily from Semantic Scholar and OpenAlex, including ~8M medical papers from the top 1,000 journals; Include abstracts and full text from open access articles, plus selective full text from partnered publishers.
tools.limitations:
**Free tier limits** - limited Pro search and 3 Deep Search per month; Quick select up to 10 papers only; Pro top 20 and Deep Search top 50
**Full-text access limits** - rely on abstracts for most paywalled papers, which can reduce extraction quality
**STEM-centric design** - make some features less suitable for qualitative and non-biomedical disciplines
**Retrieval bias** - semantic search without citation weighting tend to favor newer papers over seminal older ones
**Consensus Meter simplification** - rely on vote-counting that weighs all papers equally, which can misrepresent scientific consensus and weaken evidence synthesis
**Extraction reliability** - contain errors in AI-generated extractions and summaries, so verification still required
**Citation faithfulness** - cite real sources, but generated claims still require verification
**Deep Search not fully reproducible** – not meet formal systematic review standard, fit better for scoping reviews
tools.ai_role: Consensus prioritizes papers by relevance and research strength signals (e.g. citation counts, recency, journal reputation, study design). It implements agentic behavior through sophisticated LLM-powered components that execute multi-step, iterative searches and analytical tasks within predefined, handcrafted workflows.
tools.privacy:
No AI training on user data or user chats. 
“Consensus does not use your personal or non-personal information to train large language models. Furthermore, we do not send any user data to third parties for the purpose of training large language models.”

tools.name: Consensus - Deep Search
tools.coverage: 200M+ multidisciplinary academic papers via Semantic Scholar.
tools.ai_role: Generative LLM handles iterative query formulation, ranking judgment, and extensive synthesis.

tools.name: Consensus - MCP
tools.about: Advanced Deep Research mode of Consensus conducting thorough, iterative multi-round searches and generating comprehensive research reports.
tools.unique: Combines the "Consensus Meter" methodology with long-form, multi-round report generation
tools.similar:
Scite MCP
Wiley Scholar Gateway MCP
PubMed MCP
tools.ai_role: Enable direct integration with Claude, ChatGPT, and other MCP-compatible AI tool

tools.name: SciSpace
tools.about: An AI-powered research assistant designed to streamline literature discovery, reading, data analysis, writing, and citation generation to accelerate the research workflow.
tools.unique:
**Hybrid search** - combine keyword and semantic search
**Literature extraction table** - add custom columns (e.g. methodology) to extract and compare across papers
**Research Agent** - enable flexible agentic workflows through integration with 150+ tools and databases, including PubMed, Google Scholar, and arXiv (2025-07)
**Chat with PDF** - explain text, equations, tables, and decode figures, charts and diagrams
**Deep Review** (Pro tier) - automate literature reviews by consulting 1000+ papers and generate step-by-step review reports
**AI writing suite** - support manuscript drafting with an AI writer, paraphraser, citation generator, and AI detection
tools.similar:
Elicit (literature extraction table, systematic review)
Scite Assistant (literature search + summary)
Consensus (literature search + summary)
Undermind (agentic deep search)
tools.coverage: Covers 280M+ academic papers across all disciplines. Includes abstracts from Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, Google Scholar, and 50M+ open access full-text articles.
tools.limitations:
**Free tier limits** - restrict usage to 100 monthly credits; no Deep Review or data export; consult 20 papers by default and summarize only the top 5 results
**Full-text access limits** - rely on abstracts for most paywalled papers, which can reduce extraction quality
**Non-deterministic Results** - produce different paper rankings and summaries across repeated runs of the same query
**Retrieval bias** - semantic search without citation weighting tend to favor newer papers over seminal older ones
**Citation faithfulness** - cite real sources, but generated claims still require verification
**Extraction reliability** - contain errors in AI-generated extractions and summaries, so verification still required
tools.ai_role: SciSpace Agents has an Agent Gallery that shows tasks like systematic reviews, citation mapping, and other analyses, along with each agent’s workflow. It behaved more like a flexible general-purpose LLM, reasoning through tasks, identifying relevant papers, and using tools to find related work and run analyses.
tools.privacy: User data may be used for service improvement, analytics, and advertising. Uploaded PDFs are not used for AI training; no explicit opt-out for chat data used in training.

tools.name: SciSpace Deep Review
tools.about: SciSpace's 'Deep Review' mode performing iterative deep search and AI analysis to produce comprehensive literature review reports.
tools.unique: Integrates seamlessly with SciSpace's robust reading and table extraction tools
tools.similar:
Elicit Research Report
Consensus Deep Research
tools.coverage: 200M+ papers across all academic disciplines.
tools.limitations:
Premium feature requiring subscription
Processing time for thorough reviews

tools.name: SciSpace Agents
tools.about: SciSpace's agentic research framework enabling autonomous multi-step workflows — including paper discovery, reading, table extraction, and synthesis — within a single orchestrated session.
tools.unique: Combines SciSpace's full tool suite (search, PDF reader, table extractor) into an autonomous end-to-end research pipeline
tools.coverage: 200M+ academic papers across all disciplines via SciSpace's index.
tools.limitations:
Premium feature
Agentic workflows may require user oversight for quality control
tools.ai_role: Agentic LLM orchestrates multiple tools (search, read, extract, compare) autonomously to complete complex research tasks.

tools.name: Ai2 Asta - Find Papers
tools.about: Developed by Allen Institute for Artificial Intelligence (Ai2), Ai2 Asta is an open-source platform to help researchers find papers, synthesize evidence across multiple studies, and answer complex scientific questions. It integrates Find Papers (formerly Paper Finder), Generate a Report (formerly ScholarQA) and Analyze Data (formerly DataVoyager) into an iterative workflow that mirrors expert research practices.
tools.unique:
**Iterative multi-step search** - Decompose queries into sub-criteria, run reformulations across indices, and follow forward and backward citations
**LLM-based relevance judgment** - Evaluate each candidate paper against semantic sub-criteria and provide a short rationale for its ranking
**Full transparency** - Display every step of the search process, including query analysis, workflows, sub-criteria, and relevance judgments
**Deep Search mode** - Offer a fast mode for quick results and a work-harder mode for more exhaustive reformulation and citation tracking
**Evidence-first pipeline** - Focus on finding supporting evidence before writing the answer
tools.similar:
Undermind (iterative deep search, agentic approach)
PaperQA2 (iterative deep search, agentic approach)
Elicit – Research Report/Systematic Review (systematic review-oriented deep search with extraction tables)
Semantic Scholar (underlying data source with a simpler search interface)
tools.coverage: Covers 108M+ abstracts and 12M+ open access full-text papers from Semantic Scholar, with strong coverage in computer science, medicine, biology, and other arXiv /OA-heavy fields. It supports full-text search for open access papers and abstract-level search across the broader Semantic Scholar corpus.
tools.limitations:
**Full-text search limit** - limit full-text search to open-access papers only
**Query interpretation** - perform weakly for queries about author, venue, year, or institutional affiliation
**Workflow flexibility** - rely on rigid predefined workflows and limit conversational multi-turn interaction
**Response time** - take 2-3 minutes in “work harder” mode
**Proprietary LLM dependency** - rely on closed-source proprietary models (e.g. Claude-3.7) in production, while allow model substitution in the open-source code
tools.ai_role:
**Find papers** uses LLMs extensively for query intent parsing, query reformulation, relevance judgment, and ranking, with its primary output being a ranked list of papers with rationales
**Report generation** involves quote extraction, answer outlining and section clustering with relevant quotes, and section-by-section report generation with inline citations
tools.privacy:
Ai2 collects user prompts and search queries as “User submissions” to improve services and conduct research.

“Ai2 believes that it is important to be able to train models using User Content, since this can help advance scientific research. Accordingly, unless we specify otherwise, Ai2 reserves the right to train its models using User Content.”

tools.name: Undermind
tools.about: An AI-powered agentic research tool designed to automate and accelerate expert-level scientific literature search and review workflows.
tools.unique:
**Clarifying query** - ask follow-up questions before search to refine ambiguous research topics
**Iterative / agentic search** – conduct multiple-step searches, adapt strategy based on previously retrieved results
**Citation graph traversal** - follow forward and backward citation trails to uncover papers beyond keyword or semantic search
**High recall** - retrieve 150-200 papers for comprehensive coverage
**Timeline & Categories** - organize results into chronological and thematic sections
**Topic Match score** - help users assess result completeness by estimating the total no. of relevant papers on a topic
**Ask Expert** - answer follow-up questions across all retrieved papers
**Smart alerts** - monitor new publications and send updates
tools.similar:
Ai2 Asta – Find Papers (iterative, exhaustive search)
Elicit (deep research)
SciSpace (deep research)
Consensus (deep research)
tools.coverage: Search Semantic Scholar in real time, a database of 225M+ academic papers from PubMed, arXiv, bioRxiv, and many other sources across disciplines. It searches titles and abstracts for most papers and can analyze the full text of open-access articles available in Semantic Scholar.
tools.limitations:
**Free tier limits** - limited searches (5 per month), limited results shown/summaries; analyze abstracts, not full texts
**Search speed** – take 3-8+ minutes per search, making it less suitable for fast exploratory searches
**Database coverage** - rely entirely on Semantic Scholar, with no access to content outside its index, including paywalled full texts
**Transparency** - provide little visibility into which queries it run, which citations it follow, or why it select certain papers
**Reproducibility** - prevent exact replication of search steps, limiting use in formal systematic reviews
**Citation bias** - favor highly cited and seminal papers, which may underrepresent niche or very recent literature
tools.ai_role: An agent-like academic search that iteratively mimics a human researcher's process of searching, evaluating, and refining. Generally offers high retrieval quality among deep search tools.
tools.privacy: Non-Personal Information may be used for service improvement. No explicit opt-out for AI training.

tools.name: PaperQA2
tools.about: Developed by FutureHouse, an open-source, agent-based academic search system that employs iterative search methods and leverages LLMs to evaluate relevance and generate comprehensive answers.
tools.unique: Offers multiple search types and specialist agents (Crow, Owl, Falcon) for transparent reviews
tools.coverage: Covers ~38M papers on PubMed, 500k+ clinical trials, and open access papers from the Semantic Scholar/S2ORC corpus (which indexes 200M+ papers), with a primary disciplinary focus on biology and biomedical sciences.
tools.limitations:
Self-hosted deployment requires technical setup
Quality depends on paper accessibility and corpus coverage
tools.ai_role: Retrieval and synthesis via generative LLM deep search.

tools.name: Wiley AI Scholar Gateway
tools.about: Developed by Wiley, an AI-native research intelligence platform that provides a single Model Context Protocol (MCP) endpoint, connecting Wiley’s peer-reviewed scholarly content directly into general AI tools such as Claude, Mistral AI's Le Chat.
tools.unique: Enables AI to pilot-test and refine Boolean strategies directly against publisher infrastructure
tools.coverage: Covers 3M+ peer-reviewed articles from more than 1,300 Wiley journals, with disciplinary coverage spanning sciences, healthcare, business, law, and humanities. Future expansion is planned to include additional publisher partners (Sage, ASM, and others).
tools.limitations:
Requires Claude Pro or Le Chat
Full-text may depend on subscription
tools.ai_role: Acts as a content access layer; the connecting LLM handles query formulation and synthesis.

tools.name: Semantic Scholar
tools.about: Developed by the Allen Institute for Artificial Intelligence (Ai2), a free, AI-powered academic search engine that indexes 233M+ papers from all fields of science and serves as a key open infrastructure layer for other AI search tools.
tools.unique:
**TL;DR** - Provide one-sentence summaries of millions of papers, helping users scan search results quickly
**Citation intent and influence classification** - Distinguishes between 'highly influential' citations and those used for background or methodology, and show where it appears in the paper
**Smart ranking** - Use SPECTER/SPECTER2 embeddings trained on citation networks to group related papers, helping the system find conceptually similar research from titles and abstracts even when keywords differ.
**Semantic Reader** - Offer an augmented reading interface to read arXiv papers with citation previews, key highlights, and personalized citation suggestions
**Research Feeds** - Learn what papers users care about and recommend new research to help them stay current
**Open API** - Give developers access to paper search, author matching, citation links, related-paper data, and summary fields
tools.coverage: Indexes 233M+ papers from all fields of science, with sources including PubMed, arXiv, CORE, CrossRef, IEEE, ACM, and direct publisher partnerships, providing full-text for open access papers via Semantic Scholar S2ORC corpus. (Note: Abstract coverage for closed-access Elsevier and SpringerNature papers has been significantly reduced since late 2024)
tools.limitations:
Coverage gaps in some humanities and non-English publications
No full-text access — links to publisher sources
tools.ai_role: Use AI to organize metadata, classify citations, and generate paper TL;DR summaries. It also powers semantic discovery in select features, while main search remains primarily keyword-based with reranking.
tools.privacy:
Ai2 collects user prompts and search queries as “User submissions” to improve services and conduct research.

“Ai2 believes that it is important to be able to train models using User Content, since this can help advance scientific research. Accordingly, unless we specify otherwise, Ai2 reserves the right to train its models using User Content.”

tools.name: OpenAlex
tools.about: A fully open, free scholarly metadata index that catalogs 470M+ scholarly works under a CC0 license, serving as the most open large-scale scholarly index.
tools.unique:
**Massive coverage & open data license** – Entire dataset is downloadable, with CC0-license.
**Vector/Semantic Search** - Enable embedding-based discovery of papers by meaning, not just keywords.
**Author and institution disambiguation** - Use open-source algorithms and ORCID integration to deduplicate entities at global scale.
**Multi-level topic tagging** - Apply ML models using citation and full-text signals to assign hierarchical topics to each work.
**API for agentic workflow** - Support high-throughput usage with clear pricing, a free daily tier, and comprehensive documentation.
tools.coverage: Indexes 470M+ scholarly works (articles, datasets, software, dissertations, preprints), 60M full-text PDFs, and 2B+ citation links from all disciplines globally, including PubMed, arXiv, CrossRef, and institutional repositories.
tools.limitations:
**Scholarly metadata quality** – Depend on open sources, with incomplete coverage for subscription-only journals, especially after post-2024 abstract restrictions (from Elsevier and SpringerNature).
**No AI summarization/synthesis features** – Function as a metadata index and API rather than a reading or synthesis interface.
tools.ai_role: Uses machine learning for entity disambiguation and concept tagging; embedding-based semantic search currently in beta.
tools.privacy: “We don’t sell your data. We will never sell API-key usage data to anyone, and we don’t use it for advertising.”

tools.name: Lens.org
tools.about: A free, open-access platform for searching global patents and scholarly records, with unique links from patent citations back to the scholarly works they reference.
tools.unique:
**Unified patent and scholarly search** - Combine patent and scholarly literature search, linking patent citations to scholarly works and vice versa.
**Advanced Boolean search** - Support complex queries, classification codes, and structured field search.
**Open data reuse** - Offer CC-licensed data with API and bulk download access as a major open platform.
tools.similar:
Google Patent Search
Dimensions
OpenAlex
Semantic Scholar
tools.coverage: Covers 153M+ patents from 100+ jurisdictions and 266M+ scholarly works (articles, dissertations, preprints) with 2.1B citation links, including full-text for 40M+ open access PDFs.
tools.limitations:
**No AI/LLM features for search or synthesis** - Lack semantic search, summarization, and answer synthesis capabilities, relying on lexical Boolean search.
**Scholarly metadata quality** - Depend on open sources, with incomplete coverage for subscription-only journals, especially after post-2024 abstract restrictions (from Elsevier and SpringerNature).
**Free for non-commercial use only** - Require a subscription or institutional agreement for commercial use.

tools.name: Gemini
tools.about: Developed by Google, a multimodal AI platform designed to enhance productivity through advanced reasoning, coding, and data analysis. Currently, the Enterprise version is available on trial for HKUST staff.
tools.unique:
**Largest context window** - Utilize a 1M token context window to synthesize extremely large evidence sets in one pass.
**Google Workspace integration** - Access Gmail, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Slides, PDFs, and Google Chat to incorporate personal or institutional knowledge with public web research.
**PDF and image upload** - Allow users to upload proprietary documents and combine them with web-sourced evidence.
tools.coverage: General-purpose AI with web access. No direct academic database integration.
tools.limitations:
**Requires enterprise licensing**
**No dedicated academic paper index** - Source from the open web, which may lack the coverage, authority, and metadata quality of specialized academic databases.
**Quality of output depends on prompt quality** - Perform less effectively in narrow specialist research domains.
**Hallucination risk** - Generate inaccurate or fabricated outputs in some cases.
tools.ai_role: LLM with web browsing capabilities for general synthesis. Allow integration with MCP-compatible AI tools.

tools.name: Gemini Deep Research
tools.about: Google’s autonomous AI research agent, part of the Gemini ecosystem, that conducts multi-step, iterative research across hundreds of web sources and synthesizes findings into comprehensive reports with structured sections and citations.
tools.unique:
**Autonomous multi-step iterative research** - Conduct a continuous plan-search-browse-synthesize-refine loop across hundreds of web sources, show live progress, and complete complex research in 5-15 minutes.
**Interactive Research Plan** - Present a proposed research strategy before execution, allowing users to review, add, remove, or modify the plan.
**Largest context window** - Utilize a 1M token context window to synthesize extremely large evidence sets in one pass.
**Google Workspace integration** - Access Gmail, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Slides, PDFs, and Google Chat to incorporate personal or institutional knowledge with public web research.
**PDF and image upload** - Allow users to upload proprietary documents and combine them with web-sourced evidence.
tools.coverage: Searches the open web via Google Search, focusing on publicly accessible web pages, news sites, blogs, open-access repositories, Wikipedia, and official institutional websites, with additional integration for user's Gmail, Drive, Docs, and Google Chat via Workspace.
tools.limitations:
Not directly accessible in Hong Kong
Sources from open web, not curated academic databases
tools.ai_role: Generative LLM performing iterative sub-query generation and synthesis.

tools.name: NotebookLM
tools.about: An AI-powered research tool from Google that acts as a personalized assistant by grounding its responses strictly in the specific documents you upload, such as PDFs, Google Docs, and website links.
tools.unique:
**User-uploaded documents for questions** – Allow users to upload various types of content (e.g. PDFs, text files, audio files, web pages, YouTube links), to build a custom knowledge base for questions and answers
**Multi-document summaries** – Provide direct answers with citations by synthesizing information from multiple sources
**Various output generation** – Can generate various types of outputs (e.g. FAQs, study guides, timelines, podcast-style audio, video summaries)
**High source faithfulness** – Adhere strictly to the provided sources and avoids unsupported assumptions or hallucinations
tools.limitations:
**Limited to provided content** – Mainly works with user provided materials. It does not actively search academic databases or the wider web unless providing external links as sources.
**May oversimplify complex papers** – can struggle with highly technical or complex academic texts, often giving broader, more general explanations instead of deep analysis.
tools.ai_role: Query individual documents for explanations of content, or synthesizing insights across multiple sources into coherent, cited outputs

tools.name: ChatGPT
tools.about: Developed by OpenAI, a general-purpose AI assistant supporting text, vision, code, and advanced data analysis.
tools.unique:
**Broad multimodal capabilities** - Support vision, data analysis through Code Interpreter, and file upload in one workflow.
**MCP orchestration** - Connect to and intelligently chain multiple academic MCP servers, enabling highly composable research workflows.
tools.coverage: General-purpose AI with web access. No direct academic database integration.
tools.limitations:
**No dedicated academic paper index** - Source from the open web, which may lack the coverage, authority, and metadata quality of specialized academic databases.
**Quality of output depends on prompt quality** - Perform less effectively in narrow specialist research domains.
**Hallucination risk** - Generate inaccurate or fabricated outputs in some cases.
**No direct access in Hong Kong**
tools.ai_role: Generative LLM for reasoning, writing, coding, and synthesis — no direct academic database retrieval.

tools.name: OpenAI Deep Research
tools.about: An agentic research capability built into ChatGPT by OpenAI, that uses a specialized model to autonomously conduct multi-step research tasks on the open internet and produce comprehensive, cited research reports.
tools.unique: Highly capable reasoning for novel/complex tasks; effectively detects retraction notices via broad web synthesis
tools.coverage: Searches the open web in real-time, including academic repositories (PubMed, arXiv, SSRN, publisher sites), with MCP server connectivity allowing access to authenticated institutional or subscription-based data sources.
tools.limitations:
Requires ChatGPT Pro ($200/month)
Not directly accessible in Hong Kong
Sources from open web
tools.ai_role: Purely generative LLM handling iterative query formulation, ranking judgment, and extensive synthesis.

tools.name: Azure OpenAI
tools.about: Developed by Microsoft, an enterprise cloud platform providing API access to OpenAI’s language models through Azure infrastructure. It ensures enterprise-grade security, compliance, and data governance. Currently offered to the HKUST community by ITSO.
tools.unique:
**Research RAG infrastructure** - Support grounded research over proprietary corpora through Azure AI Search, On Your Data, agentic retrieval, and GraphRAG.
**Model customization and access** - Enable fine-tuning and provide access to 11,000+ models through Azure AI Foundry.
**Deep research automation** - Offer citation-rich, web-grounded multi-step research through the Deep Research API.
**Security and compliance** - Keep data within the customer’s Azure environment with strong compliance and no training on customer data without permission.
**Scalable deployment** - Provide reserved capacity for high-volume research workflows through PTUs.
tools.limitations:
**Implementation complexity** - Require substantial developer expertise and infrastructure to build effective research applications.
**Access and cost barriers** - Require Azure access, model approval in some cases, and significant spend for high-volume use.
**Model and RAG variability** - Require ongoing tuning to manage model drift and retrieval quality.

tools.name: Claude
tools.about: Developed by Anthropic, a highly capable general-purpose AI assistant featuring web search and an optional 'Research' mode for agentic deep research.
tools.unique:
**Research mode (agentic)** - Perform extensive iterative searching with high-volume tool use, comparable to specialized academic tools.
**Extended thinking capability** - Offer deep reasoning for complex multi-step analysis in Opus models.
**MCP orchestration** - Connect to and intelligently chain multiple academic MCP servers, enabling highly composable research workflows.
**Claude Skills** - Trigger complex multi-tool research sequences through user-defined natural-language instructions without coding.
**Claude Code / Claude Code Desktop** - Provide terminal-based agentic tools for complex multi-step programming and research workflows.
**Constitutional AI training approach** - Emphasize privacy, truthfulness, and harm avoidance as core model properties.
**Large context window** - Feature a 200K token context window across flagship models.
tools.coverage: General-purpose AI with web access. No direct academic database integration.
tools.limitations:
**No dedicated academic paper index** - Source from the open web, which may lack the coverage, authority, and metadata quality of specialized academic databases.
**Quality of output depends on prompt quality** - Perform less effectively in narrow specialist research domains.
**Hallucination risk** - Generate inaccurate or fabricated outputs in some cases.
**No direct access in Hong Kong**
tools.ai_role: Generative LLM specializing in long-document analysis, research synthesis, and structured writing.

tools.name: Grok
tools.about: Developed by xAI, a conversational AI assistant integrated with X (Twitter) for real-time social media synthesis that features an 'Expert Search' capability to perform iterative multi-step web searches, reconcile conflicting data, and generate comprehensive cited reports.
tools.unique:
**Exclusive real-time access to X platform data** - Provide access to the full live X (Twitter) data stream for social sentiment analysis, trend detection, and breaking news synthesis unavailable to competitors
**Iterative multi-step research** - Generate sub-queries, fetch real-time pages, follow links, and cross-verify claims across multiple sources
**Transparent reasoning trace** - Show source selection, evidence evaluation, and logical steps to enhance trust and verifiability
**Credibility assessment module** - Filter unreliable information by cross-verifying source reliability based on user interactions and source authority
**Large context window** - Support up to a 1M token context window in Grok 3+
**Multimodal capabilities** - Include image generation, image analysis, and video generation
**Multiple response modes** - Offer Fast, Expert, Heavy, and Auto modes that adapt to query complexity
**Multi-agents (2026-03)** - Enable developers to coordinate specialized AI agents for task delegation and collaborative reasoning across complex workflows
tools.coverage: Searches the general open web in real-time with unique privileged access to the full X (Twitter) live data stream, covering news, authoritative websites, academic resources where accessible, and trending X posts.
tools.limitations:
**No dedicated academic paper index** - - Source from the open web including X posts, which may lack the coverage, authority, and metadata quality of specialized academic databases.
**Citation attribution** - Provide less clear source attribution than tools like Perplexity Pro and perform weaker on source-heavy academic research.
**X (Twitter) data bias** - Carry bias risks due to training data from a platform associated with misinformation and extremist content.
**Privacy concerns** - Raise concerns through default enrollment in X post training use and a history of GDPR violations.
**Expert search limits** - Impose limits on Expert search queries per day or month.
tools.ai_role: Generative LLM with real-time web and X/Twitter access for synthesis. Multi-agents can coordinate specialized agents for complex workflows.
