tools.privacy: Grok may use your personal information to operate and improve the service, conduct research and model training, and analyze business and usage trends.

tools.name: Grok Expert Search
tools.similar:
OpenAI Deep Research
Gemini Deep Research

tools.name: HKUST GenAI
tools.about: HKUST's institutional GenAI platform providing the university community with secure, centralized access to leading models from major providers. Offers 8HKD per month of free credit.
tools.unique:
**Institutional data privacy** - Ensure institutional data privacy.
**'Free' monthly credit** - Offer 8 HKD in free credit per month.
tools.coverage: General-purpose AI models trained on open web sources.
tools.limitations:
**No direct database access** - Lack direct access to external databases.
**Hallucination risk** - May generate inaccurate or fabricated outputs.

tools.name: Poe
tools.about: Developed by Quora, a multi-model AI chatbot aggregator platform providing a single unified interface to access over 200 AI models from various providers, alongside community-created custom bots.
tools.unique:
**Unified multi-model access** - Provide a single interface and API endpoint for 200+ AI models without requiring separate accounts or API keys.
**Side-by-side model comparison** - Let users invoke multiple models in the same conversation thread to compare outputs on the same research question.
**Custom bot creation** - Enable no-code and webhook-based bot creation for specialized research assistants and advanced integrations.
**App and tool generation** - Create simple interactive web applications from natural language descriptions for research use cases.
**Multimodal capabilities** - Combine text, image, video, audio, and web-search tools in one platform.
tools.coverage: General-purpose AI models trained on open web sources. Model capabilities vary by provider.
tools.limitations:
**No dedicated academic paper index** - Source from the open web, which may lack the coverage, authority, and metadata quality of specialized academic databases.
**Privacy and governance risks** - Route conversation data through Poe and underlying model providers without enterprise-grade data governance
**Usage and scalability constraints** - Impose usage limits on premium models
**Limited model interoperability** - Function as a front-end router rather than a true multi-agent orchestration system, so models cannot build on each other’s outputs.

tools.name: Perplexity
tools.about: Developed by Perplexity AI, Inc., an AI-powered answer engine that combines real-time web search with LLMs to generate concise, conversational answers with inline citations to web sources.
tools.unique:
**Real-time web search with citations** - Ground every answer in live web content with inline source links.
**Multi-model flexibility** - Let users choose from multiple LLMs within one platform.
**Agentic and deep research modes** - Support iterative multi-step retrieval and long-form autonomous research reports.
**Publishing and discovery tools** - Enable users to create AI-generated pages indexed within Perplexity search.
**Focus modes** - Target searches across verticals such as academic, news, Reddit, and YouTube.
**Comet browser** - Offer an AI-enhanced browser with native Perplexity integration.
tools.coverage: Open web — not limited to academic databases. Cites web pages, news, and some academic sources.
tools.limitations:
**Limited free quota for Deep Research**
**No dedicated academic paper index** - Source from the open web, which may lack the coverage, authority, and metadata quality of specialized academic databases.
**Quality of output depends on prompt quality** - Perform less effectively in narrow specialist research domains.
**Hallucination risk** - Generate inaccurate or fabricated outputs in some cases.
tools.ai_role: Generative LLM handles query formulation, retrieves web pages, and synthesizes answers with citations.

tools.name: Perplexity Deep Research
tools.about: Perplexity's Deep Research mode performing multi-step web search and synthesis to produce detailed, cited long-form research reports.
tools.unique: Excels at broad questions synthesizing news, general web, and academic literature simultaneously
tools.similar:
ChatGPT Deep Research
Gemini Deep Research
tools.coverage: Primarily operates over the open web via its proprietary PerplexityBot crawl index, with an “Academic Focus” mode specifically searching Semantic Scholar, PubMed, and arXiv. No access to paywalled content.
tools.limitations:
Limited quota for Deep Research
Sources from open web, not curated academic databases
tools.ai_role: Generative LLM handles query formulation, retrieves web pages, and synthesizes answers with citations.

tools.name: MS Copilot
tools.about: Developed by Microsoft, an AI-powered assistant ecosystem spanning three main contexts: Copilot.microsoft.com for standalone consumer/prosumer AI chat, Microsoft 365 Copilot for enterprise productivity grounded in organizational data, and Copilot in Bing for web search with AI-synthesized answers.
tools.unique:
**Microsoft Graph grounding** - Synthesize across all data a user has permission to access within the Microsoft 365 ecosystem.
**Enterprise data protection** - Keep organizational data within the Microsoft 365 service boundary and do not use it to train LLMs.
tools.coverage: For consumer Copilot, real-time open web index plus uploaded user documents; for Microsoft 365 Copilot, all organizational data accessible plus open web search; no dedicated academic paper index.
tools.limitations:
**Requires enterprise licensing** - Require an expensive Microsoft 365 Copilot add-on, limiting access to well-resourced organizations.
**No dedicated academic paper index** - Source from the open web, which may lack the coverage, authority, and metadata quality of specialized academic databases.
**Quality of output depends on prompt quality** - Perform less effectively in narrow specialist research domains.
**Hallucination risk** - Generate inaccurate or fabricated outputs in some cases.
tools.ai_role: Generative LLM with Bing web search grounding for real-time answer synthesis with inline citations.

tools.name: MS Copilot - Researcher Agent
tools.about: Microsoft 365 Copilot's Researcher Agent — a multi-step research agent powered by OpenAI's models, combining web search with organizational data (e.g., emails, SharePoint, Salesforce) to produce structured, citation-rich research reports.
tools.unique: Deep integration with Microsoft 365 suite; can leverage enterprise data alongside web sources
tools.similar:
OpenAI Deep Research
Gemini Deep Research
tools.coverage: Open web via Bing, plus optionally connected Microsoft 365 organizational data.
tools.limitations:
Requires Microsoft 365 Copilot license
Sources primarily from open web
Not yet widely available
tools.ai_role: Agentic LLM conducting multi-step web search and synthesis to produce long-form research reports.

tools.name: Primo Research Assistant
tools.about: Developed by Ex Libris/Clarivate, an AI-powered discovery tool integrated into Primo VE (HKUST’s PowerSearch) that enhances the library’s discovery experience with GenAI capabilities.
tools.unique:
**“Free” access for Primo users** - Include with existing Primo VE subscriptions at no additional cost
**Extensive source coverage** - Search 5B+ records across journal articles, books, theses, etc.
**Prefiltering** – allow filtering by content type and date range, or add refinements directly in query
**Multilingual support** - Accept non-English queries and return answers in the same language
tools.similar:
Web of Science Research Assistant (LLM converted Boolean query and search within Web of Science database)
EBSCO Natural Language Search (LLM converted Boolean query and search within EBSCOhost database)
tools.coverage: Searches 5B+ records from Ex Libris Central Discovery Index (CDI) – a shared library discovery index used by libraries worldwide, covering journal articles, conference proceedings, books, reports, theses, etc. across all disciplines, excluding content from Elsevier, JSTOR, and DataCite.
tools.limitations:
**Query transparency** – Boolean query generated not immediately visible, require users to click “View more results” to see what it searches
**LLM-generated query quality** - limit search effectiveness because the Boolean query generated by GPT-4.1-mini can be suboptimal for comprehensive searches
**Reranking scope** – only rerank the top 30 initial search results from a 5 billion record CDI, potentially missing highly relevant papers
**Summary quality** - reduce summary quality by relying only on the top 5 ranked results
**Abstract-only RAG** - limit answer depth by generating responses only from abstracts of the 5 sources, even when full text is available in CDI
**Follow-up conversation** - do not support follow-up questions
**Content coverage gaps** - exclude news and content from major sources such as Elsevier, JSTOR, and DataCite
**Content guardrails** - block legitimate academic queries on sensitive but valid scholarly topics
tools.ai_role: Use LLM (currently GPT-4.1-mini) to generate a Boolean query, re-rank top 30 results using embeddings and select the top 5, and create a summary based on these 5 sources.
tools.privacy: “Neither your input nor the licensed content are stored by the LLM and used for any purpose other than the immediate interaction session. Clarivate does not use your data to directly or indirectly train LLMs”

tools.name: Nature Research Assistant
tools.about: Developed by Springer Nature, an AI-powered research tool built for researchers, operating as a single-paper reading assistant combined with a manuscript adviser.
tools.unique:
**Publisher-agnostic** – works with any paper from any publisher, not just SpringerNature content
**Figures walkthrough** – provides detailed AI-generated descriptions of each figure in a paper, with an audio playback option for simultaneous listening and viewing
**Related papers discovery** – surfaces key foundational papers, recent citing papers, reviews, and similar studies from across the literature
**Manuscript Adviser module** – accepts uploaded draft manuscripts and provides feedback on style, structure, and substance, including suggested alternative titles and abstracts
**Missing references detection** – identifies sections in an uploaded manuscript where a supporting reference may be missing
tools.similar:
SciSpace (chat with PDF)
Elicit (paper reading and extraction)
tools.coverage: Covers 23M papers from Springer Nature, Elsevier, and major open-access publishers from 2000 to 2025. It also works with any user-uploaded paper, and uses both uploaded content and its internal corpus to discover related papers.
tools.limitations:
**No multi-paper synthesis** – cannot answer complex research questions requiring synthesis across many papers, only working on one paper at a time or surfacing related papers
**Figures walkthrough limit** – only works with SpringerNature papers
**LLM may generate incorrect or misleading information** – SpringerNature acknowledges this risk and urges checking against source material
**Coverage gap for pre-2000 literature** – its corpus only spans from 2000 to 2025
tools.ai_role: Fixed-button summarization for individual papers, and semantic search to help users explore related research.
